notes: Each pattern is a path-like input arriving in the runner from customer-controlled sources: filenames in a cloned repo, paths in .gitmodules, symlink targets, tarball entries (V2), or user-supplied identifiers. Defense: safeOpen(rel) wrapper applies fs.realpathSync() THEN startsWith(WORKDIR + sep) — defeats .. ascent AND symlink chase. NUL byte rejected pre-resolve. Symlinks at directory-walk time rejected via fs.lstatSync().isSymbolicLink(). Working dir /var/runs/<tenant-id>/<run-id>/ is mode 0700, owned by runner UID, and (M1) read-only mounted. Per Forge ingestion-limits: .git/hooks, .gitattributes, .gitmodules, .git/config never read; package-manager scripts never executed.

patterns.expected_outcome: safeOpen rejects: fs.realpathSync resolves to /etc/passwd which does not startWith WORKDIR. Throws PathEscapeError; runner emits a 'malformed_path' finding and continues.
patterns.notes: PT-1 in threat-model.md §3.4. Canonical Unix path-traversal. CVE-2016-7193 reference (Word RTF path traversal) — same class, different surface.

patterns.expected_outcome: safeOpen rejects: realpathSync collapses double slashes to single, resolves to /etc/passwd, fails startsWith check.
patterns.notes: Double-slash padding. Bypasses naive 'split on /' then 'check for ..' filters.

patterns.expected_outcome: safeOpen rejects: realpathSync handles '....' (four dots) as a literal directory name lookup; if literal '....' does not exist, ENOENT; if it does, the canonical path still escapes WORKDIR and startsWith fails.
patterns.notes: Quad-dot bypass. Documented bypass against filters that strip exactly two-dot sequences (CVE-2019-9213 family in some Java path libraries).

patterns.expected_outcome: safeOpen rejects on realpath resolution and startsWith mismatch.
patterns.notes: Legitimate-looking prefix (src/components/) followed by ascent. Naive substring filters that only check the path start get bypassed; realpath-based filters do not.

patterns.expected_outcome: Path is URL-decoded ONCE at the ingest boundary (paths inside repos should never be URL-encoded — the encoded form is suspicious). After decode the realpath escapes WORKDIR.
patterns.notes: URL-encoded ../../. PT-3 in threat-model.md §3.4. Apache Tomcat CVE-2017-12615 was rooted in inconsistent decoding.

patterns.expected_outcome: Windows-aware path canonicalizer normalizes \ to / pre-realpath; realpath then escapes WORKDIR and startsWith fails. PT-5 in threat-model.md §3.4.
patterns.notes: Windows backslash variant. Relevant for Windows CI runners (Pipeline R10). Note: Node.js on Linux treats backslash as a literal char in filenames — Windows-aware wrapper required.

patterns.expected_outcome: Mixed forward/backward slash; Windows-aware canonicalizer normalizes both forms, realpath escapes WORKDIR.
patterns.notes: Mixed slash bypass. Real-world: CVE-2020-7615 (Express.static path traversal) hit this exact pattern.

patterns.expected_outcome: Same Windows canonicalization + realpath + startsWith.
patterns.notes: Windows SAM file traversal. Target = NT auth registry hive. Worst-case escape primitive on Windows runners.

patterns.expected_outcome: safeOpen wrapper rejects pre-realpath: any path containing \x00 throws NullByteError. Defense in depth: modern Node.js (>=10) rejects NUL natively, but the wrapper re-checks. PT-4 in threat-model.md §3.4.
patterns.notes: NUL-byte path truncation. Class of bug behind CVE-2006-7243 (PHP), Ruby's path-handling pre-2010, and many file-extension-allowlist bypasses.

patterns.expected_outcome: After URL-decode at ingest, the wrapper sees \x00 and rejects.
patterns.notes: URL-encoded NUL byte. Documented bypass against allow-list extension filters.

patterns.expected_outcome: NUL rejected at wrapper.
patterns.notes: NUL-byte extension-confusion. Once stored on disk on legacy filesystems, the file would be readable as 'avatar.jpg' but pass an allowlist that only sees '.txt'.

patterns.expected_outcome: Wrapper rejects: overlong UTF-8 encoding for '.' (0xC0 0xAE) is non-shortest-form per RFC 3629 and ingest unicode-normalize step uses Buffer.from(...).toString('utf-8', { strict: true }) which rejects overlong sequences. Even if it didn't, realpath would resolve and startsWith would fail.
patterns.notes: PT-3 in threat-model.md §3.4. Overlong UTF-8 — class of bug behind CVE-2001-0500 (IIS Unicode bug), the original directory-traversal RCE. Modern wrappers MUST reject non-shortest UTF-8.

patterns.expected_outcome: Overlong 3-byte UTF-8 encoding for '.'. Strict UTF-8 decoder rejects.
patterns.notes: 3-byte overlong variant. Different code-path in some decoders — has been the actual bypass when 2-byte form was patched.

patterns.expected_outcome: Unicode NFKC normalization in the wrapper converts U+2215 (DIVISION SLASH) to U+002F (SOLIDUS); the result is the literal ../../etc/passwd and realpath escapes WORKDIR.
patterns.notes: Unicode confusable-slash. The U+2215 division slash is one of ~30 slash homoglyphs in the Unicode confusables list. CVE-2021-42574 (Trojan Source) cited inspiration.

patterns.expected_outcome: Fullwidth . (U+FF0E) and / (U+FF0F) — NFKC normalization collapses to ASCII ../../../etc/passwd; realpath escapes.
patterns.notes: Fullwidth Latin path. Same class as PT-014 but with the most-common fullwidth confusables. Real-world bypass against several PHP CMS path filters in 2020-2022.

patterns.expected_outcome: At directory-walk: fs.lstatSync('evil-symlink').isSymbolicLink() === true → wrapper skips the entry AND emits a 'symlink_rejected' finding. Even if it weren't skipped, safeOpen('evil-symlink') would resolve via realpathSync to /etc/passwd which fails startsWith. PT-2 in threat-model.md §3.4.
patterns.notes: Symlink in repo pointing to host /etc/passwd. The git clone uses -c core.symlinks=false but this is documented as insufficient (LFS pointers bite); belt is the directory-walk lstat check.

patterns.expected_outcome: Same lstat-then-skip path; even if followed, realpath escapes this run's WORKDIR and startsWith fails. Composes with the per-tenant working dir mode 0700 (cross-tenant read would fail at OS layer too).
patterns.notes: Cross-tenant symlink — the highest-impact path-traversal in a multi-tenant runner. Defense is layered: mode 0700, separate UIDs (if container UID maps per tenant), AND realpath check.

patterns.expected_outcome: Lstat-then-skip at directory walk. node_modules is in the excluded-paths set (D9) but the runner must still defend in case the exclusion list is bypassed.
patterns.notes: Symlink in node_modules pointing to host binary. CVE-2021-23413 (jszip symlink follow) cited inspiration. Even with node_modules excluded, the defense must hold.

patterns.expected_outcome: Lstat skip at walk. /proc/self/environ would leak the runner process environment (which holds the run-scoped JWT for 5 min) if read.
patterns.notes: Symlink to /proc/self/environ — exfils the runner process env. Why we minimize what we put in env: the JWT is the only env var, and it's a 5-min token. Defense in depth.

patterns.expected_outcome: safeOpen(rel) treats absolute paths by doing path.resolve(WORKDIR, '/etc/passwd') which (per POSIX path.resolve semantics) returns '/etc/passwd'; realpath confirms; startsWith fails. Throws PathEscapeError.
patterns.notes: Direct absolute path. The path.resolve() POSIX behavior where an absolute right-arg overrides the left-arg is the gotcha; the startsWith check is the catch.

patterns.expected_outcome: Absolute path; startsWith(WORKDIR) fails because WORKDIR is /var/runs/<this-tenant>/<this-run>/. Even at the OS layer, the other tenant's dir is mode 0700 with a different UID.
patterns.notes: Direct cross-tenant absolute path. The single most-load-bearing defense — caught at multiple layers.

patterns.expected_outcome: Windows absolute path on a Linux runner is treated as a relative literal filename 'C:\Windows\System32\config\SAM' (which doesn't exist); on a Windows runner the canonicalizer + startsWith fires.
patterns.notes: Cross-OS absolute path. Both OS variants must reject — Linux via realpath ENOENT, Windows via canonicalization.

patterns.expected_outcome: Windows long-path prefix (\\?\) bypasses Win32 path normalization and grants raw NT-namespace access. Wrapper detects \\?\ prefix and rejects regardless of what follows.
patterns.notes: Win32 long-path / NT-namespace prefix. CVE-2020-1207 family. Real bypass against many Windows path-canonicalization libraries.

patterns.expected_outcome: Win32 device-namespace prefix (\\.\) — wrapper detects prefix and rejects.
patterns.notes: Win32 device namespace. Grants access to physical drives and devices. Filter must catch both \\?\ and \\.\ prefixes.

patterns.expected_outcome: Forge runner: clone with core.hooksPath=/dev/null AND .git/ directory is in the excluded-paths set (D9). The runner NEVER reads from .git/. Even if it did, the runner does not EXECUTE the hook — it's a pure read-only ingestor.
patterns.notes: Triggers on git operations. Note from threat-model.md §3.4 mitigation #8: 'No package-manager execution. The runner is a pure read-only ingestor — never an executor of customer code.' This is the policy backstop.

patterns.expected_outcome: Same: .git excluded path; no hook execution; clone with core.hooksPath=/dev/null.
patterns.notes: Pre-commit hook payload — would fire on any local commit operation, but the runner doesn't commit. Belt: hooksPath=/dev/null kills it. Suspenders: .git excluded from read.

patterns.expected_outcome: .git is in the D9 excluded-paths set. The clone-time core.hooksPath override is set via -c flag at clone time (per threat-model.md §3.4 mitigation), not from .git/config.
patterns.notes: .git/config can override core.hooksPath if the runner ever re-reads it. The defense is to set the override at clone time AND never re-read .git/config.

patterns.expected_outcome: Forge runner: .gitattributes is in the D9 excluded-paths set. Clone uses -c filter.evil-payload.clean=true (or rather, no custom filters defined) AND --no-recurse-submodules. The runner does not invoke git diff / git merge on customer repos.
patterns.notes: gitattributes filter directive — CVE-2017-1000117 / CVE-2018-17456 family. A repo with a malicious filter directive could trigger code execution at clone/checkout if filters were trusted. Defense: never invoke git operations beyond the initial shallow clone.

patterns.expected_outcome: Same exclusion. Even if read, the runner does not honor working-tree-encoding directives; all files are read as binary buffers and decoded by content-type detection.
patterns.notes: Working-tree-encoding manipulation — CVE-2022-24765 reference. Forces Git to interpret files via OS-level encoding shims that have been escape vectors historically.

patterns.expected_outcome: Excluded; runner does not invoke git archive / git checkout-index operations that would respect export-* directives.
patterns.notes: Export-subst can trigger keyword substitution. Less-impactful but corpus completeness.

patterns.expected_outcome: safeOpen: realpathSync normalizes multiple slashes to single, resolves to /etc/passwd, fails startsWith(WORKDIR).
patterns.notes: PT-6 in threat-model.md §3.4. Leading-slash + multi-slash padding. Bypasses naive 'strip leading slash' filters.

patterns.expected_outcome: Forge runner: --no-recurse-submodules at clone (threat-model.md §3.4 mitigation #2 + ingestion-limits D9). .gitmodules is in the excluded-paths set; .gitmodules submodules not auto-fetched. The path-traversal in the 'path' field is irrelevant because the submodule is never materialized.
patterns.notes: Submodule path-traversal CVE class: CVE-2018-11235 (Git remote-code-execution via submodule names). The defense composes ingestion limits with this corpus.
